deep_dive.title: Geometry-Anchored Transport Framework for Exemplar-Free Class-Incremental Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Exemplar-free class-incremental learning (EFCIL) requires stable decision boundaries within a shifting feature space. While maintaining class-conditional Gaussian statistics provides a principled classification strategy, these parametric summaries remain sensitive to anisotropic representation drift. Existing methods often transport these statistics across tasks using a decoupled, post-hoc paradigm: optimizing a backbone without explicit geometric constraints can distort the legacy manifold, limiting the precision of retroactive alignment. In this paper, we formulate feature transport as an endogenous training constraint rather than a separate post-task step, presenting the Geometry-Anchored Transport Framework. First, we derive an Analytic Geometric Anchor via Mahalanobis-aligned regression to mitigate macroscopic anisotropic drift. Second, we introduce a Topology-Aware Evolution objective that regularizes localized manifold degradation while calibrating a residual network against the analytic prior. By coupling manifold evolution with transport constraints during the primary training phase, our framework mitigates evaluation errors without requiring decoupled fine-tuning. Experiments across CIFAR-100, TinyImageNet, and ImageNet-100 demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently improves upon existing post-hoc alternatives under strict exemplar-free constraints.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Exemplar-free class-incremental learning handles novel classes in open world by transporting class statistics.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无样本类增量学习通过传输类别统计处理开放世界中的新类
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无样本类增量学习通过传输类别统计处理开放世界中的新类
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无样本类增量学习通过传输类别统计处理开放世界中的新类
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses exemplar-free class-incremental learning where new classes appear sequentially without old exemplars. It proposes a geometry-anchored transport framework that enforces feature transport as an endogenous training constraint, using analytic geometry to preserve legacy manifolds. Experiments show improved stability of decision boundaries and reduced forgetting.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对无样本类增量学习场景，新类别顺序出现且无法访问旧样本。提出几何锚定传输框架，将特征传输作为内在训练约束，利用解析几何保留旧类流形，有效稳定决策边界并减少灾难性遗忘。该方法为开放世界持续学习提供了更精确的特征对齐方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对无样本类增量学习场景，新类别顺序出现且无法访问旧样本。提出几何锚定传输框架，将特征传输作为内在训练约束，利用解析几何保留旧类流形，有效稳定决策边界并减少灾难性遗忘。该方法为开放世界持续学习提供了更精确的特征对齐方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: learning algorithms that handle open world scenarios with unknown classes

deep_dive.title: ReaORE: Reasoning-Guided Progressive Open Relation Extraction Empowered by Large Reasoning Models
deep_dive.abstract: Open Relation Extraction (OpenRE) requires a model to extract unseen relations between head and tail entities from unstructured text for real-world applications. The core challenge of OpenRE lies in achieving reliable generalization to unseen relation types. Current OpenRE approaches either employ clustering techniques, which cannot generate relation labels and suffer from poor generalization, or rely on direct relation label generation via Large Language Models (LLMs), which lack sufficient discriminative capacity to distinguish easily confused relations. To address these limitations, we propose Reasoning-guided progressive OpenRE (ReaORE), a framework for performing relation extraction through coarse-to-fine relation reasoning. Specifically, ReaORE consists of two key stages: (i) relation filtering, which reasons over multiple aspects to understand relations and instances, yielding an initial relation set, and further supplements and filters relations via embedding-based similarity to ensure the target relation is included; (ii) relation prediction, which aims to predict the target relations from the above set via fine-grained comparative reasoning to better distinguish easily confused relations. Extensive experiments on two widely used OpenRE datasets demonstrate that ReaORE outperforms existing baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: discovers unseen relation types in open-world text via progressive reasoning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过渐进推理在开放世界文本中发现未见关系类型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过渐进推理在开放世界文本中发现未见关系类型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过渐进推理在开放世界文本中发现未见关系类型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: To extract unseen relations in text, ReaORE uses a reasoning-guided progressive framework that first clusters coarsely and then refines with large reasoning models, achieving reliable generalization to novel relation types in open-world settings.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对开放关系抽取中未见关系类型泛化难题，提出推理引导的渐进式开放关系抽取框架ReaORE。该框架首先进行粗粒度聚类生成候选，然后利用大推理模型进行细粒度关系推理和区分，通过粗细粒度结合克服了普通聚类模糊与LLM判别不足的问题。实验表明其能有效发现新关系类型并生成高质量标签，为开放世界信息抽取提供了新途径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对开放关系抽取中未见关系类型泛化难题，提出推理引导的渐进式开放关系抽取框架ReaORE。该框架首先进行粗粒度聚类生成候选，然后利用大推理模型进行细粒度关系推理和区分，通过粗细粒度结合克服了普通聚类模糊与LLM判别不足的问题。实验表明其能有效发现新关系类型并生成高质量标签，为开放世界信息抽取提供了新途径。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: methods for discovering novel classes in open world settings
